core_mechanism: a shared latent Gaussian random field that jointly drives two coupled generative models — the intensity of a point process governing where observations occur and a regression governing their binary outcomes — linked by a scalar loading coefficient, so a single unobserved field simultaneously explains sampling locations and outcomes and lets one test whether the sampling mechanism is informative for the outcome
brief_reason: The abstract's method joins a log-Gaussian Cox process for locations with a probit outcome regression that share one Gaussian process spatial effect scaled by a loading parameter measuring the association between the latent drivers of placement and survival, i.e. a common latent factor coupling a point-process intensity and an outcome model.
